Class: Directory

A directory.

Extendsâ€‹

  • BaseClient

Constructorsâ€‹

Constructorâ€‹

new Directory(ctx?, _id?, _digest?, _exists?, _export?, _findUp?, _name?, _sync?): Directory

Constructor is used for internal usage only, do not create object from it.

Parametersâ€‹

ctx?â€‹

Context

_id?â€‹

DirectoryID

_digest?â€‹

string

_exists?â€‹

boolean

string

string

string

DirectoryID

Directory

Methodsâ€‹

asGit()â€‹

asGit(): GitRepository

Converts this directory to a local git repository

Returnsâ€‹

GitRepository

asModule(opts?): Module_

Load the directory as a Dagger module source

Parametersâ€‹

opts?â€‹

DirectoryAsModuleOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Module_

asModuleSource()â€‹

asModuleSource(opts?): ModuleSource

Load the directory as a Dagger module source

Parametersâ€‹

opts?â€‹

DirectoryAsModuleSourceOpts

Returnsâ€‹

ModuleSource

changes()â€‹

changes(from): Changeset

Return the difference between this directory and another directory, typically an older snapshot.

The difference is encoded as a changeset, which also tracks removed files, and can be applied to other directories.

Parametersâ€‹

fromâ€‹

Directory

The base directory snapshot to compare against

Returnsâ€‹

Changeset

chown()â€‹

chown(path, owner): Directory

Change the owner of the directory contents recursively.

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Path of the directory to change ownership of (e.g., "/").

ownerâ€‹

string

A user:group to set for the mounted directory and its contents.

The user and group must be an ID (1000:1000), not a name (foo:bar).

If the group is omitted, it defaults to the same as the user.

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

diff()â€‹

diff(other): Directory

Return the difference between this directory and an another directory. The difference is encoded as a directory.

Parametersâ€‹

otherâ€‹

Directory

The directory to compare against

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

digest()â€‹

digest(): Promise<string>

Return the directory's digest. The format of the digest is not guaranteed to be stable between releases of Dagger. It is guaranteed to be stable between invocations of the same Dagger engine.

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<string>

directory()â€‹

directory(path): Directory

Retrieves a directory at the given path.

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Location of the directory to retrieve. Example: "/src"

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

dockerBuild()â€‹

dockerBuild(opts?): Container

Use Dockerfile compatibility to build a container from this directory. Only use this function for Dockerfile compatibility. Otherwise use the native Container type directly, it is feature-complete and supports all Dockerfile features.

Parametersâ€‹

opts?â€‹

DirectoryDockerBuildOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Container

entries()â€‹

entries(opts?): Promise<string[]>

Returns a list of files and directories at the given path.

Parametersâ€‹

opts?â€‹

DirectoryEntriesOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<string[]>

exists()â€‹

exists(path, opts?): Promise<boolean>

check if a file or directory exists

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Path to check (e.g., "/file.txt").

opts?â€‹

DirectoryExistsOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<boolean>

export()â€‹

export(path, opts?): Promise<string>

Writes the contents of the directory to a path on the host.

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Location of the copied directory (e.g., "logs/").

opts?â€‹

DirectoryExportOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<string>

file()â€‹

file(path): File

Retrieve a file at the given path.

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Location of the file to retrieve (e.g., "README.md").

Returnsâ€‹

File

filter()â€‹

filter(opts?): Directory

Return a snapshot with some paths included or excluded

Parametersâ€‹

opts?â€‹

DirectoryFilterOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

findUp()â€‹

findUp(name, start): Promise<string>

Search up the directory tree for a file or directory, and return its path. If no match, return null

Parametersâ€‹

nameâ€‹

string

The name of the file or directory to search for

startâ€‹

string

The path to start the search from

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<string>

glob()â€‹

glob(pattern): Promise<string[]>

Returns a list of files and directories that matche the given pattern.

Parametersâ€‹

patternâ€‹

string

Pattern to match (e.g., "*.md").

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<string[]>

id()â€‹

id(): Promise<DirectoryID>

A unique identifier for this Directory.

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<DirectoryID>

name()â€‹

name(): Promise<string>

Returns the name of the directory.

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<string>

search()â€‹

search(opts?): Promise<SearchResult[]>

Searches for content matching the given regular expression or literal string.

Uses Rust regex syntax; escape literal ., [, ], {, }, | with backslashes.

Parametersâ€‹

opts?â€‹

DirectorySearchOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<SearchResult[]>

stat(path, opts?): Stat

Return file status

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Path to stat (e.g., "/file.txt").

opts?â€‹

DirectoryStatOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Stat

sync()â€‹

sync(): Promise<Directory>

Force evaluation in the engine.

Returnsâ€‹

Promise<Directory>

terminal()â€‹

terminal(opts?): Directory

Opens an interactive terminal in new container with this directory mounted inside.

Parametersâ€‹

opts?â€‹

DirectoryTerminalOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

with()â€‹

with(arg): Directory

Call the provided function with current Directory.

This is useful for reusability and readability by not breaking the calling chain.

Parametersâ€‹

argâ€‹

(param) => Directory

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withChanges()â€‹

withChanges(changes): Directory

Return a directory with changes from another directory applied to it.

Parametersâ€‹

changesâ€‹

Changeset

Changes to apply to the directory

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withDirectory(path, source, opts?): Directory

Return a snapshot with a directory added

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Location of the written directory (e.g., "/src/").

sourceâ€‹

Directory

Identifier of the directory to copy.

opts?â€‹

DirectoryWithDirectoryOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withError()â€‹

withError(err): Directory

Raise an error.

Parametersâ€‹

errâ€‹

string

Message of the error to raise. If empty, the error will be ignored.

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withFile()â€‹

withFile(path, source, opts?): Directory

Retrieves this directory plus the contents of the given file copied to the given path.

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Location of the copied file (e.g., "/file.txt").

sourceâ€‹

File

Identifier of the file to copy.

opts?â€‹

DirectoryWithFileOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withFiles()â€‹

withFiles(path, sources, opts?): Directory

Retrieves this directory plus the contents of the given files copied to the given path.

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Location where copied files should be placed (e.g., "/src").

sourcesâ€‹

File[]

Identifiers of the files to copy.

opts?â€‹

DirectoryWithFilesOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withNewDirectory()â€‹

withNewDirectory(path, opts?): Directory

Retrieves this directory plus a new directory created at the given path.

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Location of the directory created (e.g., "/logs").

opts?â€‹

DirectoryWithNewDirectoryOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withNewFile()â€‹

withNewFile(path, contents, opts?): Directory

Return a snapshot with a new file added

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Path of the new file. Example: "foo/bar.txt"

contentsâ€‹

string

Contents of the new file. Example: "Hello world!"

opts?â€‹

DirectoryWithNewFileOpts

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withoutDirectory()â€‹

withoutDirectory(path): Directory

Return a snapshot with a subdirectory removed

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Path of the subdirectory to remove. Example: ".github/workflows"

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withoutFile()â€‹

withoutFile(path): Directory

Return a snapshot with a file removed

Parametersâ€‹

pathâ€‹

string

Path of the file to remove (e.g., "/file.txt").

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withoutFiles()â€‹

withoutFiles(paths): Directory

Return a snapshot with files removed

Parametersâ€‹

pathsâ€‹

string[]

Paths of the files to remove (e.g., ["/file.txt"]).

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withPatch()â€‹

withPatch(patch): Directory

Experimental

Retrieves this directory with the given Git-compatible patch applied.

Parametersâ€‹

patchâ€‹

string

Patch to apply (e.g., "diff --git a/file.txt b/file.txt\nindex 1234567..abcdef8 100644\n--- a/file.txt\n+++ b/file.txt\n@@ -1,1 +1,1 @@\n-Hello\n+World\n").

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withPatchFile()â€‹

withPatchFile(patch): Directory

Experimental

Retrieves this directory with the given Git-compatible patch file applied.

Parametersâ€‹

patchâ€‹

File

File containing the patch to apply

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withSymlink()â€‹

withSymlink(target, linkName): Directory

Return a snapshot with a symlink

Parametersâ€‹

targetâ€‹

string

Location of the file or directory to link to (e.g., "/existing/file").

linkNameâ€‹

string

Location where the symbolic link will be created (e.g., "/new-file-link").

Returnsâ€‹

Directory

withTimestamps()â€‹

withTimestamps(timestamp): Directory

Retrieves this directory with all file/dir timestamps set to the given time.

Parametersâ€‹

timestampâ€‹

number

Timestamp to set dir/files in.

Formatted in seconds following Unix epoch (e.g., 1672531199).

Returnsâ€‹

Directory
